body_txt: So, you have a big idea for a Legacy Character you're really itching to try, but there's just one problem: the original character is still active in your continuity, and shows little inclination to retire. Well, then. We'll just have to do something about that , won't we? Contrast You Kill It, You Bought It when a character in-story , instead of a writer, kills another character to take on his job or MacGuffin . This trope is similar to Mentor Occupational Hazard from an in-setting standpoint, but differs significantly as a trope in that such a mentor exists only to die so the hero can replace them; victims of this trope, on the other hand, are always established characters in their own right. May invoke Take Up My Sword . If the character's been absent for a while, they might have to be brought Back for the Dead (or just suffer a Bus Crash ). Beware: This is an easy way to create a Replacement Scrappy in some fans' eyes.
